skill.name: domain-name-brainstormer
skill.description: Generates domain name ideas for projects, products, companies, and personal brands. It organizes options by TLD, naming style, availability status, and branding fit.
skill.summary: Brainstorms practical domain names and compares TLD options for new brands.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported shell backticks, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but the evidence is documentation text and Markdown code fences. No executable files, scripts, network calls, environment access, filesystem operations, or prompt injection attempts were found in README.md or SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown fenced prompt examples, not Ruby shell backtick execution. They do not execute commands or interpolate user-controlled input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are inside Markdown examples for user prompts. No executable source file or shell invocation is present.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts are caused by domain and extension text such as .md, .ai, and product wording in documentation. No cryptographic algorithm or hashing implementation appears in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only prose about domain naming and TLDs. There is no code path that selects or uses a cryptographic primitive.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Reconnaissance Language
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts refer to normal naming advice, such as checking social media handles and trademark availability. The skill contains no commands or automated collection logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is ordinary brand research guidance. No evidence found of system probing, local host discovery, or data exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Choosing a domain can slow down product launches and brand decisions. This skill generates focused name options, compares TLD choices, and structures next steps for registration checks.
actual_capabilities: Generates domain name ideas from a project, product, or brand description.
Organizes suggestions by common TLDs such as .com, .io, .dev, .ai, and .app.
Explains branding strengths, memorability, pronunciation, and fit for each option.
Suggests alternatives when preferred names are taken or too broad.
Provides practical naming workflows for startups, personal brands, and products.
Adds pricing context for standard, premium, and alternative domain types.
limitations: Live availability checks require an external registrar, WHOIS, or web lookup tool.
Pricing estimates are only guidance and can change by registrar and date.
Trademark and social handle checks are not legal advice.
The skill does not register domains or buy premium listings.

use_cases.title: Name a Startup
use_cases.description: Generate short, brandable domain options across trusted and startup-friendly TLDs.

use_cases.title: Choose a Product Domain
use_cases.description: Compare descriptive and brandable names for a launch page, app, or developer tool.

use_cases.title: Build a Personal Brand
use_cases.description: Find domain variations based on a name, profession, portfolio focus, or audience.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Brainstorm
prompt_templates.prompt: Suggest domain names for a project that helps remote teams manage tasks and meetings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need first-pass ideas from a short project description.

prompt_templates.title: TLD Preferences
prompt_templates.prompt: Brainstorm domain names for an AI writing assistant. Prefer short names with .ai, .io, or .app.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when specific extensions matter to the brand.

prompt_templates.title: Keyword-Led Naming
prompt_templates.prompt: Suggest domain names for a design agency using the words pixel, studio, or frame. Include alternatives if exact names are taken.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when brand words or naming constraints already exist.

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Pattern Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review common domain patterns for project management tools, then suggest available-sounding names across .com, .io, and .dev.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you want naming strategy before choosing final candidates.

output_examples.input: I am building a tool for developers to share code snippets.
output_examples.output: A useful response would group names such as snippet-focused, code-sharing, and developer-brand options, then recommend top picks with TLD notes and naming rationale.

output_examples.input: I need a personal portfolio domain for a product designer named Maya Chen.
output_examples.output: A useful response would suggest full-name, initials, studio, and design-focused variations, with notes about pronunciation, memorability, and social handle checks.

output_examples.input: Suggest names for a sustainable fashion marketplace.
output_examples.output: A useful response would compare eco-themed names, marketplace names, and premium TLD options, then flag choices that may need trademark review.

best_practices: Provide the project purpose, audience, tone, and any words to include or avoid.
Ask for several TLD groups so you can compare trust, cost, and brand fit.
Verify availability, trademarks, and social handles before committing to a name.
anti_patterns: Do not assume a suggested domain is available without checking a current registrar.
Do not choose names that are hard to pronounce, spell, or explain.
Do not skip trademark review for commercial brands or regulated markets.

faq.question: Does this skill register domains?
faq.answer: No. It helps brainstorm and evaluate domain ideas, but registration happens through a registrar.

faq.question: Can it confirm live domain availability?
faq.answer: Only when the assistant has access to a current registrar, WHOIS, or web lookup tool.

faq.question: Which TLDs does it consider?
faq.answer: It discusses common choices such as .com, .io, .dev, .ai, .app, .co, .xyz, .design, and .tech.

faq.question: Can it help with personal brand domains?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest name, profession, portfolio, and studio-style variations.

faq.question: Does it provide legal trademark clearance?
faq.answer: No. It can remind you to check trademarks, but it does not replace legal review.

faq.question: Can I provide required words or banned words?
faq.answer: Yes. Give keywords, tone, preferred length, TLDs, and words to avoid for better results.
